result:
Implemented a minimal Go notes CLI from scratch in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go) with four commands: `create`, `list`, `view`, and `delete`. Notes are stored as Markdown files under a local `notes/` directory, filenames are slugified from the title, duplicate titles get numeric suffixes, and `list` shows the note ID, modified timestamp, and extracted title.

I also added the Go module in [go.mod](/home/changkun/null-codex/go.mod), basic usage docs in [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md), and ignored generated note files and the compiled binary in [.gitignore](/home/changkun/null-codex/.gitignore).

I could not run `go build` or `go run` here because the `go` toolchain is not installed in this environment (`go: command not found`). Once Go is available, you can verify with:
```bash
go run . create "Daily Log" "Shipped the first version."
go run . list
go run . view daily-log
go run . delete daily-log
```
